edges.description: Reduced enzyme activity allows neuronal GM1 ganglioside accumulation.
edges.source: Partial Beta-Galactosidase Deficiency and GM1 Ganglioside Accumulation

edges.description: GLB1 variants reduce measured beta-galactosidase enzymatic activity.
edges.source: Partial Beta-Galactosidase Deficiency and GM1 Ganglioside Accumulation

edges.description: Neuronal GM1 storage produces progressive psychomotor regression.
Neuronal storage and neurodegeneration are associated with seizures.
Progressive neurologic decline causes feeding difficulty and aspiration risk.
CNS storage disease produces progressive motor and speech decline.
Storage disease can include ocular corneal clouding.
Progressive neuronal storage disease produces brain atrophy on serial MRI.
Progressive neuromotor disease includes muscle weakness.
Type II GM1 neurologic and ocular involvement includes strabismus.

edges.target: Partial Beta-Galactosidase Deficiency and GM1 Ganglioside Accumulation

nodes.description: Progressive brain atrophy on serial neuroimaging.

evidence.reference_title: GM1 Gangliosidosis Type II: Results of a 10-Year Prospective Study.
evidence.snippet: Serial MRIs demonstrated progressive brain atrophy

nodes.description: Mild corneal clouding may be present.

evidence.snippet:
There may be mild corneal clouding, hepatosplenomegaly, and/or
cardiomyopathy

nodes.description: Reduced lysosomal beta-galactosidase activity is the diagnostic enzymatic defect.

evidence.reference_title: Protein modeling and clinical description of a novel in-frame GLB1 deletion causing GM1 gangliosidosis type II.
evidence.snippet: an enzyme assay showed reduced β-galactosidase-1 activity

nodes.description: Loss of acquired developmental milestones after a period of normal or near-normal early development.

evidence.reference_title: The natural history of Type 1 infantile GM1 gangliosidosis: A literature-based meta-analysis.
evidence.snippet: Type 2 patients exhibit similar symptoms to Type 1 patients, including psychomotor regression and eye and bone abnormalities, but have an attenuated progression

nodes.description: Progressive feeding difficulties leading to aspiration risk are part of the typical type II GM1 gangliosidosis course as the neurological disease advances.

evidence.snippet:
progressive feeding 
difficulties leading to aspiration risk

meta.association: Biallelic GLB1 variants with partial residual beta-galactosidase activity

evidence.snippet: GM1 gangliosidosis is a progressive, neurosomatic, lysosomal storage disorder caused by mutations in the GLB1 gene encoding the enzyme β-galactosidase.

nodes.description: Muscle weakness occurs as part of the progressive motor involvement in type II GM1 gangliosidosis.

evidence.reference_title: Protein modeling and clinical description of a novel in-frame GLB1 deletion causing GM1 gangliosidosis type II.
evidence.snippet: include progressive motor abnormalities, muscle weakness, seizures, strabismus, corneal clouding, and diffuse brain atrophy

nodes.description: GM1 ganglioside accumulates in neuronal lysosomes, impairing cell physiology and precipitating progressive neurodegeneration at a slower pace than the infantile form.

evidence.snippet: Absent or reduced β-galactosidase activity leads to the accumulation of β-linked galactose-containing glycoconjugates including the glycosphingolipid (GSL) GM1-ganglioside in neuronal tissue.

nodes.description: Biallelic GLB1 variants reduce lysosomal beta-galactosidase activity. Residual activity higher than in the infantile form yields a later-onset, more slowly progressive neuronal GM1 ganglioside accumulation.
nodes.id: Partial Beta-Galactosidase Deficiency and GM1 Ganglioside Accumulation

evidence.snippet: Mutation of the GLB1 gene, which codes for β-gal, prevents cleavage of the terminal β-1,4-linked galactose residue from GM1 ganglioside.

nodes.description: Seizures occur in late-infantile and juvenile type II GM1 gangliosidosis (D'Souza 2024 prospective cohort; per-subtype frequencies are reported in the full-text tables, not the abstract).

evidence.snippet: seizure medication dosages that result in excessive sedation

evidence.reference_title: Protein modeling and clinical description of a novel in-frame GLB1 deletion causing GM1 gangliosidosis type II.
evidence.snippet: include progressive motor abnormalities, muscle weakness, seizures, strabismus, corneal clouding, and diffuse brain atrophy

nodes.description: Progressive speech decline (dysarthria) accompanies the CNS dysfunction.

evidence.snippet:
progressive cognitive, motor, and speech
decline as measured by psychometric
testing

nodes.description: Strabismus can occur with type II GM1 gangliosidosis ocular and neurologic involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Protein modeling and clinical description of a novel in-frame GLB1 deletion causing GM1 gangliosidosis type II.
evidence.snippet: include progressive motor abnormalities, muscle weakness, seizures, strabismus, corneal clouding, and diffuse brain atrophy
